title: crotalaria cobalticola
intro: Crotalaria cobalticola is a type of flowering plant, specifically a member of the pea family (Fabaceae), native to tropical regions of Africa. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to remove mercury and arsenic from the body, making it a potential treatment for heavy metal poisoning. Crotalaria cobalticola has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as skin infections, wounds, and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
